title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Client Identity, and Proxy Trust · Go Semantics Case: Forwarded Header Trust Chain
summary: An origin accepts arbitrary Host values, constructs password-reset links from untrusted forwarded fields, rate-limits a proxy address, and logs raw targets containing secrets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo forwarded header trust chain increment in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Accept Forwarded or X-Forwarded values only from configured proxy hops and derive client, scheme, and host with a documented traversal rule.
Validate authoritative host and port against a configured allowlist before routing, link construction, cache keys, or tenant selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
